front.title: Applying BGP-LS Segment Routing over IPv6(SRv6) Extensions to BGP-LS-SPF

author.organization: China Satellite Network Innovation Research Institute Co., Ltd.

abstract.t: For network scenarios such as Massively Scaled Data Centers (MSDCs), BGP is extended for Link-State (LS) distribution and the Shortest Path First (SPF) algorithm based calculation. BGP Link State Shortest Path First (BGP-LS-SPF) Routing leverages the mechanisms of both BGP protocol and BGP-LS protocol extensions. Segment Routing over IPv6 (SRv6) provides a source routing mechanism that allows a flow to be restricted to a specific topological path, while maintaining per-flow state only at the ingress node(s) to the SRv6 domain. In some networks, it may be useful to enable SRv6 based source routing mechanism together with BGP-LS-SPF. This document proposes to introduce the BGP Link-State (BGP-LS) extensions for SRv6 to the BGP-LS-SPF SAFI to enable SRv6 capabilities in BGP-LS-SPF. The usages and formats of these extensions are also provided in this document.
